items.front: What governs ATC service in the North American ICAO Region?
items.back: Use the procedures and minima in Section 8-10.

items.front: How is vertical separation provided in the North American ICAO Region?
items.back: Use Chapter 4 IFR altitude assignment and verification plus facility directives that depict the transition between flight levels and metric altitudes.

items.front: What distance-based longitudinal minimum may be used in the North American ICAO Region when DME reports or radar observations establish the spacing?
items.back: Use 30 NM; otherwise use at least 40 NM when based on RNAV.

items.front: What lateral minima apply in the North American ICAO Region by navigation approval level?
items.back: Use 50 NM for RNP 4 or RNP 10 approved aircraft, 23 NM for approved aircraft in the Anchorage Oceanic and Continental CTAs, and 90 NM for aircraft not covered by those provisions.
